tipRules.tip: KVD: In clear water, use dice/bug baits (Sexy Bug, Tumbleweed) on a Neko rig — incredible action and visibility. In stained water, stick to the traditional Neko worm for a subtler profile. Superglue the tungsten weights so they don't fly out when fish jump.

newOpinions.defaultTip: KVD: Every serious angler needs a Neko rig. Wacky-hook through the middle, weight in the nose for straight fall. Superglue weights in place. Lead wire wrapped on the hook shank adds subtle weight without bulk. Use 8lb braid to 8lb fluoro leader.

tipRules.tip: KVD: Drop shot with dice/bug baits (Sexy Bug, Tumbleweed) is a game-changer. Use the lightest weight possible — 1/8oz ideal. The slow fall with all that action is what makes it deadly. Great during Mayfly hatches. Fish annihilate it on the fall.

knowledge.insight: KVD: Three weight tricks for Neko rigs: (1) Superglue all tungsten Neko weights — they won't fly out when fish jump. (2) Wrap lead wire from fly tying department (Bass Pro) around the hook shank for subtle extra weight without bulk. (3) Put weights in both ends of creature baits like the Sexy Bug — hook in the middle, weight in both nose and tail.

KVD: GS1 spinning rod, 6'10" medium action for accuracy around docks. Longer spinning rod for open water distance. 8lb braid to 8lb fluoro leader. Mustad Apex weedless hook for cover/docks, exposed hook in open water. Mustad Apex drop shot hook (wide gap) for creature baits.

KVD: Dice/bug baits on a drop shot are a new frontier. Lighter weight = better (1/8oz ideal, 1/4oz max in deeper water). The slow sinking action is the key — once on bottom you can still shake it traditionally, but most bites come on the fall. Game-changer for clear water smallmouth and during Mayfly hatches.

KVD: Neko-rigged creature baits are 'deadly springtime bait around the spawn.' In clear water, most fish bite on the initial fall — if they're on a bed, the bait lands next to them and they eat it immediately. Works for both largemouth and smallmouth/spots.
